stem:
Diadromous fish migrate between freshwater and marine biomes during their life cycle. The migration’s obligate nature is why diadromous fish can be _______ those that are merely euryhaline (able to tolerate high salinity): the euryhaline blackchin tilapia can survive high salinity, but its life cycle does not involve relocation to a different biome, as does that of the diadromous wild salmon.

Which choice completes the text with the most logical and precise word or phrase?
explanation: To be “demarcated from” something is to be set apart or distinguished from it. The passage stresses a difference — diadromous fish must migrate between biomes, whereas euryhaline fish merely tolerate salinity — so the two groups can be told apart. They aren’t “reconstituted as,” “conflated with” (merged with), or “derived from” one another.
